summary:
With an overall rating of 85/100, Blueberry Futures is a futures-focused evaluation platform under the Blueberry family of brands, offering two one-step challenges with clear trading rules. Blueberry Futures offers $25K, $50K, $100K, and $150K accounts on both Ascent and Accelerated programs. You can trade CME futures covering equity futures, micro equity futures, FX futures, livestock, and micro crypto on the BlackArrow trading terminal, available via desktop app, web terminal, and mobile app.

Blueberry Futures is part of the Blueberry family of brands. The setup is straightforward — two one-step challenges, identical targets across all account sizes, and a choice between the more forgiving Ascent (end-of-day drawdown) or the tighter Accelerated (real-time trailing drawdown). Fees start at $129 for the Accelerated $25K account and $139 for the Ascent $25K account. No activation fees are charged. All accounts operate in a simulated trading environment.
pros: Part of the Blueberry family of brands
Simple one-step challenges with clear rules
Level 1 CME data included, no activation fees
CME market selection including micro crypto (MBT, MET)
BlackArrow available on desktop, web, and mobile
cons: Payout caps and buffer reduce flexibility
bestFor: Futures traders who value clear rules, fast execution, and the operational backing of an established brand family
notIdealFor: Grid traders, heavy martingale users, and anyone who depends on automation or needs multiple platform options

verdictBlocks.heading: Blueberry Futures Final Score
verdictBlocks.body:
Blueberry Futures lands at 85/100 overall, helped by strong challenges, competitive pricing, CME market access including micro crypto, and the operational backing of the Blueberry brand family. Blueberry Futures is an educational and evaluation platform, not a broker. It suits traders who already understand futures behaviour and want a clean, rules-based simulated trading environment.

Ascent is a good fit if you like EOD-style risk with room to hold through noise, while Accelerated appeals more to disciplined intraday traders who are comfortable protecting equity highs. Account sizes range from $25K to $150K on both programs, with fees starting at $129 (Accelerated) and $139 (Ascent). No activation fees are charged.

Traders become eligible for live-account review after 7 successful payout cycles or $28,000 in total withdrawals from one funded account. If approved, the live funded amount equals the original plan's max drawdown: $25K → $1,000, $50K → $2,000, $100K → $3,000, $150K → $4,500. Eligibility does not guarantee approval.

faq.a: Blueberry Futures is part of the Blueberry family of brands, which includes Blueberry Markets and Blueberry Funded. However, Blueberry Futures itself is an educational and evaluation platform, not a broker. While it benefits from the wider ecosystem's operational infrastructure, the futures program should not be confused with the regulated brokerage entity. Blueberry Futures is still building independent review history.

faq.q: What are the trading costs on Blueberry Futures?
faq.a: Level 1 CME data is included with all accounts, and no activation fees are charged. Level 2/DOM data is optional at $61/month for funded traders. The challenge fee covers full access to BlackArrow and all supported markets. Payout withdrawals are processed through RiseWorks and USDT on TRC-20, with Veriff KYC required before the first payout.
A full refund is available only if no trade has been placed and the request is made within 14 days of purchase. After trading starts, refunds are not available.

Yes, prop trading is high risk because strict drawdown limits mean any breach ends the account immediately. In futures, that risk feels sharper due to fast price movement and contract volatility. The difference compared to trading your own capital is that your financial risk is capped at the initial challenge fee and any resets, rather than losses coming directly from your personal funds.
